chief_complaint: A 76-year-old mainland Chinese man presents with progressive hoarseness.
history: The patient has chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and initially presented with hoarseness, likely due to recurrent laryngeal nerve involvement. Contrast-enhanced chest computed tomography revealed a mass in the left hilar and upper mediastinal regions, suggestive of lung carcinoma. The patient was treated with intravenous amphotericin B followed by oral isavuconazole, resulting in marked lesion regression and symptomatic improvement after 6 months of antifungal therapy.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings are not provided.
labs: Computed tomography-guided mediastinal biopsy confirmed the presence of nonseptate, ribbon-like hyphae, consistent with mucormycosis. Contrast-enhanced chest computed tomography revealed a mass in the left hilar and upper mediastinal regions.
